subject: Statistics
title: Seasonality: Repeating Patterns
description: Learn Seasonality: Repeating Patterns in one minute.

hook.text: Ice cream sales peak every July. Flu cases spike every January. The calendar drives repeating data patterns.

buildup.text: Seasonality is a regular, predictable pattern that repeats at fixed intervals — weekly, monthly, or yearly.

discovery.text: Seasonal adjustment removes these predictable swings so you can see the underlying trend more clearly.

twist.text: Not all repeating patterns are seasonal. A business cycle of 5-7 years is cyclical, not seasonal — its period varies.

climax.text: Governments seasonally adjust economic data before release. The unemployment rate you see is already corrected.

punchline.text: Strip the seasons and the true signal appears. Every winter spike is expected.

quiz.question: What is the difference between seasonal and cyclical patterns?
options: Seasonal patterns repeat at fixed intervals; cyclical patterns have variable, unpredictable periods
Cyclical patterns only occur in economic data
